title: New filter captures and recycles aluminum from manufacturing waste
summary: MIT engineers developed a nanofiltration process that can capture aluminum ions from manufacturing waste streams and potentially upcycle them back into production. In lab tests, the membrane selectively captured more than 99% of aluminum ions while allowing other ions to pass through, which could reduce hazardous waste and improve yield.
